sectionHeader.title: What Is ADHD Coaching?
sectionHeader.subtitle: A thousand colorful bricks. One puzzling question: why won't I start?

subsections.title: A View from the Rug
defaultParagraphs: You're sitting on the floor surrounded by a thousand colorful bricks. You know exactly what you want to build — a perfect 1,000-piece masterpiece. Well, 997. You lost three pieces somewhere.

But you don't start. Not because you don't know how. Not because you don't want to. You just… sit there. Completely aware of everything you need to do, and completely unable to make yourself do the first thing.
This isn't laziness. It's a disconnect between knowing and doing — and it's one of the most disorienting parts of having an ADHD brain.

subsections.title: What Is ADHD Coaching?
defaultParagraphs: Coaching isn't someone building the set for you. It's having a Master Builder sit beside you on the rug.

Therapy might explore why you're afraid of the Expert-level sets. Medication clears the clutter off the table so you can actually see the pieces. Coaching is the manual — written for your brain, by you. Not productivity hacks. Not glue. A sorting system you actually want to use.

subsections.title: How Coaching Works
defaultParagraphs: Think of sessions as a weekly Sorting Sync.

We look at what's on your table: what's in progress, what's stalled, and what's been sitting in the corner since January. Together we break the 100-page instruction manual into five-minute micro-builds. We take stock of your mental energy, decide which bag to open first, and figure out what's actually blocking the build — not what should be blocking it.
I'm not there to judge the mess on the floor. I'm there to help you find the techniques that work for your brain.
adhdParagraphs: We break the overwhelming stuff into five-minute micro-builds
We figure out what's actually blocking you — not what should be blocking you
We look at what's in progress, what's stalled, and what's been sitting in the corner since January
No judgment. Just the right techniques for your brain

subsections.title: What Coaching Can and Can't Do
defaultParagraphs: A coach is a precision tool — like a brick separator. It makes the hard parts easier. It doesn't do the snapping for you.

Coaching can help you find a better way to organize the pieces. It won't replace therapy, and it won't replace medication. We can't change the pieces you were given — it's a non-standard set — but we can change the way you arrange them.
What coaching will do is help you figure out an arrangement that actually works — and help you stop waiting for the "right" moment to start.
adhdParagraphs: ✅ Helps you stop waiting for the "right" moment to start
✅ Changes the way you arrange what you've got
❌ Can't  the pieces you were given
❌ Can't help you win the lottery

subsections.title: Adapt to the Way Your Brain Works
defaultParagraphs: "Just be more organized" is like being told to build a castle without any baseplates. Technically possible. Deeply miserable. Not the point.

We don't force your brain through a linear 1-to-100 manual if you work better building the dragon first. We honor how your brain actually moves — hyperfocus, creative leaps, nonlinear everything — and design your Build Station around that. The pieces you need most? Always within reach.

subsections.title: Self-Awareness + Self-Compassion Come First
defaultParagraphs: You step on a stray brick in the dark. You curse the brick. You turn on the light and see the mess. You curse yourself. You meant to put them away. You didn't.

Here's the thing — everyone leaves bricks out. Everyone. But for us, it never feels like a minor oversight or an understandable remnant after an exhausted day. It feels like proof. Proof that we are the mess, not the floor.
That's the part worth examining — not the brick, not even how the mess came to be — but the story we've been telling ourselves about what that means.

subsections.title: When Coaching Makes Sense
defaultParagraphs: You're ready when the to-build pile is touching the ceiling and you're tired of just looking at the boxes. What used to bring joy now just brings dread.
You don't need more sets. You need a system to open the ones you already have.
Coaching is for the builder who's done waiting for the "perfect" moment — and is ready to see something on the shelf.
adhdParagraphs: 🧱 The to-build pile is touching the ceiling
📦 You're tired of just looking at the boxes
😶 What used to bring joy now just brings dread
🔧 You're not sure you're ready — but you're tired of waiting to feel ready
